mnemonics.title: Good Hash Function Properties
mnemonics.content: D-U-F-S: Deterministic, Uniform, Fast, Simple - these make a hash function shine!

mnemonics.title: Collision Resolution Methods
mnemonics.content: Separate Chains Link Lists; Open Address Probes - S.C.L.L.O.A.P. remembers the two main categories!

mnemonics.title: Linear vs Quadratic Probing
mnemonics.content: Linear adds i (step by 1), Quadratic adds i² (square the step) - same concept, different step size!

acronyms.expansion: Collision Per Unit - think of load factor as collisions per bucket
acronyms.usage: Remember: higher load factor = more collisions = worse performance

acronyms.expansion: Open Addressing - OA techniques: Linear, Quadratic, Double hashing
acronyms.usage: Used when all elements must fit in the main array

acronyms.expansion: Primary Analysis - when α > 0.7, performance drops

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a coat check room with numbered hangers. The hash function is like a rule that tells each person (key) which hanger number (index) to use. Multiple people wanting the same hanger = collision!
Think of each hash table bucket as a train car. When multiple keys hash to the same car, they form a linked list (like people standing in that car).
Like finding an empty parking spot: if your spot is taken, check the next one, then the next, until you find space.
Like checking parking spots at increasing distances: 1st, 4th, 9th, 16th spots away (squares: 1², 2², 3², 4²).
Think of a restaurant with tables (buckets) and customers (elements). Load factor = % occupancy. Above 70% occupancy, service slows down - time to open more tables!

analogies.analogy: A hash table is like a dictionary where words (keys) are mapped to definitions (values) using a smart indexing system - you don't flip through pages, you go directly to the right page!
Two different people getting the same locker number at a gym - they need to work out who gets what (collision resolution).
Moving to a bigger house when your family grows too large - you need to reorganize everything in the new space!
In a crowded mall during sales, everyone clusters around popular stores, making the crowd thicker in certain areas - making it harder to move through.
A hash function is like a fingerprint scanner - it converts a person (key) into a unique-looking code (hash) that identifies where they belong.
